channel.title: Comments for page "Memento Vivere"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Memento Vivere"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I must say that I deeply appreciate the perversion of Alexandria you've constructed here.

Between the unusual geography, the librarians, the rules against fire, the dark corners, and the general difficulties associated with accessing information, it mirrors Alexandria well… but flips all of it.

Alexandria's geography is perfect, while this isn't. Alexandria's custodians care deeply about their archives and those detailed therein, while these librarians are broken and heartless. Alexandria is warm and inviting and the darkness is a place of quiet comfortable contemplation, while the darkness here hides abominations. The post of Watcher is an important and sacred task, if mostly symbolic, while the Mother is purely symbolic and doesn't do anything.

It also makes one wonder what has happened to the original Alexandria on Earth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if it's the asteroid belt in our solar system

It isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And so humans came to the asteroid belt

We travelled how many lightyears to get here,

If it's the asteroid belt in our solar system, and they traveled from Earth (or anywhere else within our solar system) they would have traveled much, much less than one light year. "Millions of miles" would be the more appropriate phrase here (even if you're talking about the Kuiper belt).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Part 2 has arrived! Bit of a shift in focus but the next chapter will be much more character-focused and much less weird-gothic-ness; I wanted to use this chapter to do some worldbuilding about this series' version of the Foundation and the fragmentation/loss of identity that is now creates.
